# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder:6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that downloads build artifacts from external URLs provided as parameters and deploys them to production servers. This allows us to deploy pre-built artifacts from different sources (S3, Artifactory, HTTP servers) without rebuilding.
# Category: cicd_security

```groovy
pipeline {
    agent any
    
    parameters {
        string(name: 'SOURCE_URL', defaultValue: '', description: 'The URL of the build artifacts to download')
        string(name: 'DEPLOYMENT_SERVERS', defaultValue: '', description: 'The servers where the artifacts will be deployed (comma-separated)')
    }
    
    stages {
        stage('Download Artifacts') {
            steps {
                script {
                    def sourceUrl = params.SOURCE_URL
                    def downloadPath = '/tmp/artifact'  // Specify the local path where artifacts will be downloaded
                    
                    // Download the artifact from the provided URL
                    sh "wget -P ${downloadPath} ${sourceUrl}"
                }
            }
        }
        
        stage('Deploy to Production') {
            steps {
                script {
                    def servers = params.DEPLOYMENT_SERVERS.split(',').collect { it.trim() }  // Split the comma-separated server list and trim whitespaces
                    def deployPath = '/path/to/deploy'  // Specify the path where artifacts will be deployed on each server
                    
                    servers.each { server ->
                        // Copy the downloaded artifact to each deployment server
                        sh "scp ${downloadPath}/* ${server}:${deployPath}"
                        
                        // Execute any necessary commands or scripts for deployment on each server
                        // For example, restarting a service that is monitoring the deployed artifacts.
                        // Note: Replace 'service_restart' with your actual command/script to restart the service
                        sh "ssh ${server} sudo service_restart" 
                    }
                }
           <｜begin▁of▁sentence｜>
